Every app open now checks for a fresh copy of the HTML. If the network is unavailable, it falls back to the cached version. All other assets (icons, manifest) remain cache-first for performance. The new cache name cardiaclens-v9.10.267 also forces the old worker to be replaced on first load.
🧪 New in v9.10.266 — June 17, 2026
  • Secure Access — three bug fixes. (1) Migration guard: if the device had old single-letter Secure Access data (CL_SEC_DONE set but CL_SEC_COLOR missing from the previous PIN-only system), all CL_SEC_* keys are now cleared on first load so the user goes through fresh setup with the new color-letter keyboard instead of being stuck. (2) Grid state reset: window._clCurrentGrid is now explicitly nulled before calling _clResetKbState() in both _clShowLock and _clLockFromBtn, ensuring a fully fresh keyboard is built every time the lock screen opens. (3) Setup screen spacing: reduced tagline margin, card padding, and step-bar margin so the 3-step setup flow fits on small iPhone screens without clipping or awkward vertical centering; container now uses align-items:flex-start with margin:auto on the inner wrapper so tall content scrolls cleanly instead of fighting the centered layout.
🧪 New in v9.10.274 — June 16, 2026
  • Secure Access — Color-Letter Keyboard. Replaces the text-input PIN system with a randomized 4×6 grid of letters and numbers. Each open, the grid reshuffles. Unlock sequence: (1) tap your chosen letter first, then (2) tap any 3 of 4 keys sharing your secret color (the other 20 keys show 7 other colors as misdirection). Setup: 3 steps — pick a letter, pick a color, set a recovery question. Lockout: 3 failed sequences → 30-second cooldown; 6+ → 5-minute cooldown with Forgot link. Recovery: correct secret-question answer forces a full reset before granting access.
🧪 New in v9.10.274 — June 16, 2026
  • Guard Dog — beta-friendly overhaul. Five changes to reduce noise for beta testers: (1) Dismiss now clears the queue entirely — if a tester dismisses the banner, it stays gone. Beta testers as a group are the safety net; one report reaching support is enough, and re-alarming everyone who dismissed trains them to ignore it. New issues after dismissal queue fresh and re-show the banner. (2) Startup re-show logic simplified — since dismiss clears the queue, anything present on reload is genuinely unsent. (3) AudioContext constructor now wrapped in try/catch — iOS throws NotAllowedError when constructed at script load without a prior user gesture; this was hitting Guard Dog on every session start. (4) audioContext.resume() in unlockAudioContext now has a .catch() — iOS can reject this call; unhandled rejection was going to Guard Dog. (5) speechSynthesis.speak() in _speechFallback now wrapped in try/catch — iOS blocks speech synthesis without a user gesture; the resulting exception was the "Failed to start the audio device" report in Guard Dog. Also added .catch() to Notification.requestPermission() — some iOS versions reject instead of resolving with "denied".
🧪 New in v9.10.263 — June 15, 2026
  • Guard Dog — duplicate report suppression. The same version mismatch message was being reported repeatedly every 5 minutes for hours — 7 identical reports in one session. Root cause: _gdQueue had no duplicate check and queued every call unconditionally. Fixed: before queuing any report, _gdQueue now checks whether an entry with the same type and message is already in the queue. If it is, the duplicate is silently dropped. Guard Dog reports each distinct issue exactly once until the queue is cleared by sending a report.
🧪 New in v9.10.262 — June 15, 2026 Once a user had completed Secure Access setup, tapping "Maybe Later" on the first-time prompt still unlocked the app, bypassing the lock entirely. Root cause: _clSkipSetup called _clUnlock unconditionally regardless of whether setup was complete. Fixed: _clSkipSetup now checks CL_SEC_DONE — if setup is complete the lock is mandatory and Maybe Later is silently ignored. Additionally, the Maybe Later button is now hidden entirely when setup is complete so it never appears as an option to someone who has already established a key letter.
🧪 New in v9.10.261 — June 15, 2026 Three issues addressed before iPhone deployment: (1) Floating 🔓 button now uses env(safe-area-inset-bottom) so it sits above the iPhone home indicator instead of behind it. (2) PIN input now uses autocomplete="new-password" to suppress iOS keychain suggestions that would appear for type="password" fields. (3) Tapping anywhere on the PIN box now focuses the input — works around iOS Safari's restriction on programmatic focus calls that aren't triggered by direct user gesture.
🧪 New in v9.10.260 — June 15, 2026 Added explicit "Not case sensitive" callout — uppercase or lowercase both unlock. Also corrected the unlock steps which still referenced the removed "Tap here" tap area and the removed security question login step. Added note that 3 wrong attempts auto-triggers the security question for recovery.
🧪 New in v9.10.259 — June 15, 2026 The CardiacLens icon in the main header was still referencing icon-192.png which does not exist when running from a local file, causing a broken image next to a doubled "CardiacLens CardiacLens" text. Replaced with base64 embedded real icon — now renders correctly locally and deployed.
🧪 New in v9.10.258 — June 15, 2026 v9.10.257 replaced icon-192.png references with a hand-drawn SVG approximation that looked nothing like the actual CardiacLens icon and also corrupted the main app header causing "CardiacLens CardiacLens" to appear doubled. Fixed: all 8 lock screen icon references now use the actual CardiacLens icon (heart + magnifying glass) embedded as a base64 data URI — renders correctly locally and deployed, no external file required. Main header icon-192.png reference restored and untouched.
🧪 New in v9.10.257 — June 15, 2026 The lock screen was referencing icon-192.png which only exists on the deployed server, causing a broken image when running from a local file. Replaced all 8 instances (7 lock screen panels + 1 Help Security mockup) with an inline SVG of the CardiacLens heart+magnifying glass icon — renders correctly everywhere, local or deployed, no external file required.
🧪 New in v9.10.256 — June 15, 2026 Welcome tab now lists the Security tab in the navigation guide. Data Management tab now has a blue callout pointing users to Secure Access before clearing or sharing data. All relevant Help tabs (Welcome, Getting Started, FAQ, Privacy, Security, Data Management, Known Issues, Support) now reference Secure Access. Feature tabs (Sentinel, Ask, Breathing Logger, etc.) correctly have no mention — Secure Access is not relevant to those tools.
🧪 New in v9.10.255 — June 15, 2026 The Security tab now shows a styled mockup of exactly what the lock screen looks like, with a callout explaining what a stranger sees versus what the authorized user does. Removes any ambiguity about the PIN deception.
🧪 New in v9.10.254 — June 15, 2026 All 7 lock screen panels were showing a red heart emoji ❤️ instead of the CardiacLens icon (icon-192.png). Replaced with the actual app icon across every panel — setup steps, PIN screen, recovery, and key reveal.
🧪 New in v9.10.253 — June 15, 2026 (1) Input is now type="password" — nothing visible when typing, character is processed and cleared instantly. (2) After 3 wrong key letters, the security question automatically appears — no need to find and tap "Forgot your code?". Wrong attempt counter resets on unlock or when lock screen reopens. (3) "Forgot your code?" is now 16px, bold, navy blue with underline — clearly tappable.
🧪 New in v9.10.252 — June 15, 2026 Input field placeholder said "Type your key letter" — defeating the entire deception by telling anyone who sees the screen exactly what the unlock mechanism is. Changed to "Enter PIN".
🧪 New in v9.10.251 — June 15, 2026 Previously the correct letter triggered a security question as a second step. That was unnecessary friction — the letter is the key, one factor is enough. Correct letter now unlocks the app directly. Security question is now used only for recovery (forgot key letter), not as a login step.
🧪 New in v9.10.250 — June 15, 2026 When a stranger types numbers on the lock screen, each digit now fills one of the four PIN dots exactly as a real PIN pad would. After 4 digits the screen shakes and resets — the stranger believes they entered the wrong PIN and tries again or gives up. They never suspect a letter is required. Previously numbers were silently ignored, making the screen look broken and hinting that it wasn't a normal PIN pad.
🧪 New in v9.10.249 — June 15, 2026 The key letter input had color:#f0f4f8 — the exact same color as the background — making every character typed invisible. Combined with caret-color:transparent hiding the cursor, the input appeared completely unresponsive even though it was accepting input correctly. Users had no way to know anything was being entered. Fixed: text is now visible in navy blue at 28px, cursor is visible, placeholder says "Type your key letter". Also fixed: autocapitalize="characters" so mobile keyboards default to uppercase, matching how keys are stored.
🧪 New in v9.10.248 — June 15, 2026 Two bugs: (1) If the user had previously dismissed the first-time setup prompt, the lock screen showed the PIN input but CL_SEC_DONE was never set, so every key letter typed was silently rejected. Fixed: lock screen now always shows the setup prompt when CL_SEC_DONE is not set, regardless of whether the first-time flag was previously seen. (2) Change Key Letter in Settings could set CL_SEC_KEY without CL_SEC_DONE, leaving the app in an inconsistent state where a key existed but setup was not considered complete. Fixed: Change Key Letter now blocks if setup has not been fully completed and directs the user to the proper setup flow.
🧪 New in v9.10.247 — June 15, 2026 The "Tap here, then type your code" area did not activate keyboard input on Chromebook. Root cause: the input was hidden and required a click to proxy focus to it, which does not work reliably on desktop Chrome. Fixed: replaced the hidden input + tap area with a single visible input field that auto-focuses when the lock screen appears. No clicking required — open the app, start typing your key letter immediately. Works on both Chromebook keyboard and iPhone tap.
🧪 New in v9.10.246 — June 15, 2026 Added explanation of the 🔓 bottom-left floating button to the Security help tab: what it is, where it lives, and that one tap locks the app immediately.
🧪 New in v9.10.245 — June 15, 2026 When Secure Access is active, a floating blue 🔓 button now appears permanently in the bottom-left corner of the app. One tap locks the entire app immediately and returns to the PIN decoy screen. The button is always visible so users can lock and walk away without hunting through menus. Hidden when Secure Access is not set up. Replaces the previous header icon approach which was too easy to miss.
🧪 New in v9.10.244 — June 15, 2026 When Secure Access is active, a 🔒 icon now appears in the header version row next to the clock. Tapping it immediately locks the entire app and returns to the PIN decoy screen. The icon is hidden when Secure Access is not set up — no clutter for users who haven't enabled it. Icon appears automatically after setup completes and disappears after a security reset.
🧪 New in v9.10.243 — June 15, 2026 A new Security tab now appears in Help between Privacy and Data Mgmt. Covers: what Secure Access is, what it protects against and what it does not, step-by-step setup instructions, how to unlock the app, how to recover a forgotten key letter, how to change or reset the key, and guidance on sharing the key with a trusted person.
🧪 New in v9.10.242 — June 15, 2026 Added Secure Access guidance to three Help locations: Getting Started Tips (new blue tip box explaining the feature and pointing to Settings → 🔒 Secure Access), FAQ (three new Q&A entries covering what Secure Access is, what to do if you forget your key letter, and what it protects against), and Privacy tab (new Secure Access section between Airplane Mode and Who Can See Your Data).
🧪 New in v9.10.241 — June 15, 2026 CardiacLens now opens to a PIN decoy screen on every launch. The PIN screen accepts a letter, not a number — anyone who doesn't know this types numbers and never gets in. First-time users are prompted to set up a key letter and security question on first launch, with a Maybe Later option. Setup is three steps: choose a key letter, choose a security question, confirm with an explanation of how the PIN decoy works. Recovery is via tapping "Forgot your code?" three times to trigger the security question. Settings → Secure Access allows changing the key letter or resetting entirely (with warning). The lock screen protects the entire app — no data is visible until unlocked.
🧪 New in v9.10.240 — June 15, 2026 A floating ⬆ button now appears in the bottom-right corner of any long data page after scrolling down more than 300px. Tapping it smoothly scrolls back to the top of that page — where the period selector, action buttons, and close button live. Applies universally to all modal-content pages: Medication Effectiveness, Procedure Impact, Advanced Analytics, Deep Dive, HR Rate Detector, Decompensation Warning, Pulse Pressure, Sentinel, Pinned Events, Help/FAQ, and any other long-scrolling modal. Button disappears automatically when scrolled back near the top or when the modal is closed. No per-page changes required.
🧪 New in v9.10.239 — June 15, 2026 Ask was failing on every request because the worker URL was set to cardiaclens-ask.sommers-texan.workers.dev (which does not exist) instead of clinbridge-ask.sommers-texan.workers.dev (the actual deployed worker). The URL was incorrectly updated during the June 13 rebrand. Fixed.
🧪 New in v9.10.238 — June 15, 2026 Doses logged as “Rosuvastatin 20 mg” (space before mg) were not matching the medicine list entry “Rosuvastatin 20mg” (no space) — causing 32 legitimately logged doses to be invisible to the adherence calculation and producing a false 20% adherence rate. Root cause: the name comparison used .toLowerCase() only, without stripping whitespace. Fix: a _normMedName() helper now normalizes both sides — lowercase + collapse all internal whitespace — before comparing. Affects all three medication categories (scheduled, PRN, periodic). Any medication whose logged name has inconsistent spacing relative to the medicine list entry will now match correctly.
  • Medication adherence — “Prior dose” badge direction corrected. The “Prior dose” badge was appearing on active medications that have a linkedTo field set (e.g. Metoprolol 12.5mg, the current dose). It should only appear on discontinued medications with a link (e.g. Metoprolol 25mg, the superseded dose). Fixed: isLinkedPredecessor now requires both linkedTo set AND status === 'discontinued'.
    • 🧪 New in v9.10.237 — June 15, 2026 Advanced Analytics now calculates each medication’s adherence only against the days it was actually active in the analysis period. Previously, expectedDoses used the full window for every medication regardless of start or stop date, causing stopped medications (e.g. Losartan discontinued June 4) to accumulate missed-dose counts for every day after stopping, and dose changes (e.g. Metoprolol 12.5mg replaced by 25mg) to show two medications each penalised for the days the other was active. Fix: expectedDoses is now computed from med.startDate and med.discDate (both already stored) — only days within the medication’s active window inside the analysis period are counted. Missed-dose detection likewise skips dates outside the active window. Stopped medications show a red “Stopped [date]” badge; mid-period starts show a green “Started [date]” badge; dose-link predecessors (linkedTo set) show a purple “Prior dose” badge and are excluded from the Overall Daily Med Adherence total to avoid double-counting the same drug. No data structure changes — uses fields already present on every medication record.
  • Ask — offline banner. When Ask fails due to a network error or Cloudflare Worker outage, a persistent red banner now appears at the top of the Ask panel: “⚠️ Ask is currently unavailable — your data and all monitoring features are working normally” with a Retry button. The banner is dismissed automatically on the next successful Ask response. Previously a failure produced only a transient chat bubble that was easy to miss and gave no persistent indication that the service was down.
    • 🧪 New in v9.10.236 — June 14, 2026
    • Guard Dog personality and false alarm elimination. The heartbeat indicator now has four distinct states, each meaning exactly one thing: 🐕 Eyes open… — fresh install or cleared storage, Guard Dog just arrived and getting its bearings; 🐕 On patrol… — app just opened, provisional heartbeat written, first real health check running in 8 seconds (clears automatically); 🐕 H:MM AM/PM — all clear, last verified patrol time; 🐕 Guard Dog fell asleep (red) — no verified health check in 30+ minutes, something is genuinely wrong. Previously “Guard Dog fell asleep” appeared on every app resume from background because iOS suspends timers when backgrounded, making the stored timestamp stale even though Guard Dog was healthy — it cleared itself in seconds but trained the developer to ignore it, defeating its own purpose. Fixed with two mechanisms: (1) a provisional heartbeat write at startup so the indicator never flashes “fell asleep” during the 8-second initialization window; (2) a separate CL_GD_HEARTBEAT_verified key written exclusively by the real health check, allowing “On patrol…” and the real time to be distinguished from each other unambiguously. Staleness threshold increased from 10 to 30 minutes to account for iOS Low Power Mode timer suspension. “Guard Dog fell asleep” now only appears when it genuinely means something.
    🧪 New in v9.10.235 — June 14, 2026
    🧪 New in v9.10.234 — June 14, 2026
    🧪 New in v9.10.233 — June 14, 2026
    🧪 New in v9.10.232 — June 14, 2026
    🧪 New in v9.10.231 — June 14, 2026
    🧪 New in v9.10.230 — June 14, 2026
    🧪 New in v9.10.229 — June 14, 2026
    🧪 New in v9.10.228 — June 14, 2026
    • BP save — Before/After validation no longer blocks on iOS. Tapping Save without selecting Before Med or After Med triggered a native alert() dialog whose Close button did not resume app execution on iOS. Replaced with _bpShowError() inline red banner above Save button.
    🧪 New in v9.10.227 — June 14, 2026
    • Morning Check-In data loss fix. The v9.10.226 migration used a longest-array-wins rule for CARDIACLENS_MORNING_CHECKIN. Any check-in logged after the June 13 rebrand was silently overwritten by the longer ClinBridge history. Fixed: migration now performs a date-keyed union — every entry from both sides is preserved, CardiacLens (newer) record wins same-date conflicts. Also: FLUID_EVENTS_SNAPSHOT conflict now picks the side with the newer savedAt date.
    • Analytics badges now appear immediately on startup. Badges were deferred 5 000 ms on cold-start and BFCache resume paths. Fixed: fire at 100 ms / 200 ms respectively.
    🧪 New in v9.10.226 — June 14, 2026
    • ClinBridge → CardiacLens data migration (critical). All users who ran CardiacLens before the June 13 rebrand had their data stored under clinbridge_* and CLINBRIDGE_* localStorage keys. After the rebrand, the app read exclusively from cardiaclens_* / CARDIACLENS_* keys — so pinned events, breathing sessions, saved meals, appointment notes, sentinel events, Ask history, Med Intel, baseline conditions, timeline snapshots, anchor investigations, and morning check-in history were all silently invisible. Root cause: no migration was written at rebrand time. Fix: a one-time migration block runs at the top of block 1 (earliest script execution) on every device. It checks for a CL_REBRAND_MIG_V1 guard key — if absent, it copies every unmigrated clinbridge_*/CLINBRIDGE_* key to its cardiaclens_*/CARDIACLENS_* counterpart. Also merges the legacy CB_PINNED_EVENTS key into cardiaclens_pinned_events, deduplicating by source+eventDate. Guard key set once; migration never re-runs.
    🧪 New in v9.10.225 — June 13, 2026
    • Beta flow polished. Got It button now correctly dismisses the welcome overlay. Active beta testers see a dedicated modal with two options: Send Feedback Now (opens email) or Leave Beta Program (clears enrollment with confirmation). Standard users see the original routing modal unchanged.
    • Morning Check-In data loss fix. The v9.10.226 migration used a longest-array-wins rule for CARDIACLENS_MORNING_CHECKIN. Any check-in logged after the June 13 rebrand (stored in the CardiacLens key) was silently overwritten by the longer ClinBridge history, making the prompt revert from “Edit” back to “Start →”. Fixed: migration now performs a date-keyed union of both arrays — every entry from both sides is preserved, with the CardiacLens (newer) record winning when the same date appears in both. Also: FLUID_EVENTS_SNAPSHOT conflict now correctly picks the side with the newer savedAt date rather than defaulting to ClinBridge.
    • Analytics badges now appear immediately on startup. All four badge groups were deferred 5 000 ms on both cold-start and BFCache resume paths even though data was already in memory. Fixed: badges fire at 100 ms on cold-start, 200 ms on BFCache restore.
    🧪 New in v9.10.226 — June 14, 2026
    • ClinBridge → CardiacLens data migration (critical). All users who ran CardiacLens before the June 13 rebrand had their data stored under clinbridge_* and CLINBRIDGE_* localStorage keys. After the rebrand, the app read exclusively from cardiaclens_* / CARDIACLENS_* keys — so pinned events, breathing sessions, saved meals, appointment notes, sentinel events, Ask history, Med Intel, baseline conditions, timeline snapshots, anchor investigations, and morning check-in history were all silently invisible. Root cause: no migration was written at rebrand time. Fix: a one-time migration block runs at the top of block 1 (earliest script execution) on every device. It checks for a CL_REBRAND_MIG_V1 guard key — if absent, it copies every unmigrated clinbridge_*/CLINBRIDGE_* key to its cardiaclens_*/CARDIACLENS_* counterpart. Also merges the legacy CB_PINNED_EVENTS key into cardiaclens_pinned_events, deduplicating by source+eventDate. Guard key set once; migration never re-runs.
    🧪 New in v9.10.225 — June 13, 2026
    • Beta flow polished. Got It button now correctly dismisses the welcome overlay. Active beta testers see a dedicated modal with two options: Send Feedback Now (opens email) or Leave Beta Program (clears enrollment with confirmation). Standard users see the original routing modal unchanged.
    🧪 New in v9.10.224 — June 13, 2026
    • Send Feedback — all buttons now work. Root cause found and fixed: all feedback modal functions (openSupportEmail, openBetaEnrollScreen, closeFeedbackModal, closeBetaEnrollModal, confirmBetaEnroll) were defined in a script block that browsers do not execute. All functions moved to window.* properties in the first-executing script block and verified with headless browser functional tests before release.
    🧪 New in v9.10.223 — June 13, 2026
    • Send Feedback button fix (root cause). openFeedbackFlow() was defined late in the script load order, so taps on the button found the function undefined and silently failed. Fixed by promoting openFeedbackFlow to window.openFeedbackFlow in the earliest-running script block, guaranteeing it is always available on first tap.
    🧪 New in v9.10.222 — June 13, 2026
    • Send Feedback button fix. Tapping the button now correctly opens the feedback modal on iOS Safari. Root cause: iOS does not apply flex layout properties set while display:none — fixed by switching modals to visibility:hidden/visible with pointer-events management.
    🧪 New in v9.10.221 — June 13, 2026
    • Send Feedback button. A purple "Send Feedback" button now appears on the main screen for all users. Standard users can report an issue (routes to support@cardiaclens.com) or volunteer to become a beta tester. Active beta testers go directly to the weekly feedback email. Graduated testers see a thank-you screen with support contact only — no re-enrollment.
    • In-app beta enrollment. Any user can now join the beta program directly from the app. Enrollment is a one-way commitment: two consecutive ignored weekly check-ins = graduated, no re-enrollment. Graduation state is tracked in localStorage and synced back to the enrollment record.
    🧪 New in v9.10.220 — June 13, 2026
    • QR code updated. The Welcome tab QR code now correctly encodes https://cardiaclens.com (previously pointed to the old domain).
    🧪 New in v9.10.219 — June 13, 2026
    • App header icon. Replaced the generic bar-chart emoji in the main header with the CardiacLens heart+magnifying glass icon (icon-192.png), matching the PWA install icon.
    🧪 New in v9.10.218 — June 13, 2026
    • CardiacLens. The app has a new name and home: cardiaclens.com. Same app, same free cardiac health monitoring built by a patient for patients — just a new identity, icon, and license (PolyForm Noncommercial). No data is affected; install the app fresh from cardiaclens.com to get the new branding and icon.
    🧪 New in v9.10.217 — June 12, 2026
    • Help documentation accuracy — Meal Builder description updated to match v9.10.215's save/update behavior. The feature-history text describing Meal Builder said "Save to Library saves the meal permanently" without distinguishing between saving a new meal and updating one you arrived at via ✏️ Edit. Updated to describe both paths: editing an existing saved meal and re-evaluating now updates that meal in place (button reads 💾 Update), while building a meal from scratch adds a new entry to the library.
    🧪 New in v9.10.216 — June 12, 2026
    • Meal Builder — fixed iOS auto-zoom that compounded with each field (second bug found via the beta feedback channel, same report). The "Add Item" form's fields (food name, prep method, Amount, Serving, Sodium, Potassium, Phosphorus) and the meal name/type fields all used font sizes between 12px and 14px. iOS Safari/PWA automatically zooms the viewport in whenever a focused input or select has a font size under 16px — and with several small-font fields in sequence, each focus compounded the zoom further, leaving the screen progressively more zoomed in with no way back except a manual pinch-to-zoom-out. All nine fields are now 16px, the standard iOS zoom-prevention threshold, so filling out a new food item no longer triggers (or compounds) the zoom.
    🧪 New in v9.10.215 — June 12, 2026
    • Manage Meals — editing a saved meal, evaluating it, then saving created a duplicate instead of updating the original (first bug reported through the new beta feedback channel). Root cause: analyzeMeal() (the "Evaluate" button) builds mbPendingMeal from the current items but never recorded which saved meal — if any — was being edited (mbEditingId). After the AI analysis returned, tapping "💾 Save to Library" in mbSaveLastMeal() always pushed a brand-new entry with id: Date.now(), regardless of whether the user had arrived there via "✏️ Edit" on an existing meal. The original meal was left unchanged with its old ingredients, while the edited version landed in a new, easy-to-miss duplicate entry — reported as "lost my new ingredients." Separately, "✏️ Modify & Re-analyze" (mbModifyLastMeal → _loadPendingMealIntoBuilder) called openMealBuilder() without the 'edit' flag, which explicitly resets mbEditingId to null — so even a second round of edit → evaluate → modify lost the edit context. Fixed by carrying editingId: mbEditingId through mbPendingMeal: mbSaveLastMeal() now updates the original entry in place (preserving its id and saved date, updating items/totals/lastEvaluated) when an editingId is present, and only falls back to creating a new entry for meals built from scratch; _loadPendingMealIntoBuilder() restores mbEditingId after openMealBuilder() and relabels the Save button "💾 Update" accordingly.
    🧪 New in v9.10.214 — June 12, 2026
    • Beta feedback email — fixed inconsistent line spacing in the body template. The mailto: link itself worked correctly, but in iOS Mail, the "Device & OS" and "Anything else on your mind" prompts (and the trailing 📷 emoji) collapsed onto a single line while every other question kept its blank-line separation — despite the source template using identical \n\n\n spacing throughout. Rather than rely on blank-line spacing that can collapse unpredictably, the template now numbers each prompt (1–5) with single blank lines between them, so every question stays visually distinct even if spacing collapses somewhere. The screenshot reminder is now its own labeled line ("📷 Screenshot: ...") rather than trailing into the previous question.
    🧪 New in v9.10.213 — June 12, 2026
    • Beta tester: report something the moment you find it, without waiting for the weekly check-in (/beta only). Previously the only way to send feedback was through the weekly check-in modal or the "Preview Check-In Now" button in Settings — both tied to the check-in schedule. Added a standalone _betaReportNow() path that opens the same guided email template immediately, with zero effect on the skip counter or next check-in date — the weekly rhythm continues unaffected. Two entry points: (1) the purple "BETA BUILD" banner at the top of every screen is now tappable, with updated text "tap to report something"; (2) a new "📨 Report Something Now" button in Settings → Beta Tester, above the existing preview button.
    🧪 New in v9.10.212 — June 12, 2026
    • Beta tester weekly check-in & feedback program (/beta only). The beta channel exists for active feedback — this version adds the mechanism. Roughly weekly, a modal asks "Anything to report this week?" with two real responses: Give Feedback (opens a pre-filled email to betatesting@cardiaclens.com with guided prompts — what you were doing, what you expected, what happened, device/OS, plus a reminder to attach a screenshot) and Nothing to Report (a valid response — just confirms you're still using it). Either response resets the skip counter and reschedules for another week, indefinitely — active testers never see anything else. Dismissing without responding ("Remind me later") counts as a skip; two consecutive skips immediately show a graduation overlay thanking the tester and linking to cardiaclens.com, with no further prompts. Since /beta and the main site share the same browser storage, graduating requires no data export or migration — it's the same data, just a different URL going forward. New Settings section (Beta Tester, /beta only) shows current week number, skip count, and next check-in date, plus a "Preview Check-In Now" button that triggers the real prompt immediately for testing — any response counts as the actual weekly check-in. New functions: _isBetaBuild(), _betaCheckInMaybeShow(), _betaCheckInRespond(), _betaCheckInSkip(), _betaFeedbackMailto().
    🧪 New in v9.10.211 — June 12, 2026
    • Help & About search — tapping a result showed only the nav bar, with the matched section collapsed to nothing. Root cause: setHelpSection() hides inactive sections using six properties (display, visibility, height, overflow, padding, margin), all !important — but _helpShowAllSections() and helpGoToResult(), used by the search feature, only ever toggled display. When a search result was tapped, the target section's display was restored to block, but its leftover visibility:hidden, height:0, and overflow:hidden from a prior tab switch were never cleared — so the section was technically "shown" but visually collapsed to zero height. This also explained why Prev/Next did nothing: the highlighted <mark> elements existed and the counter was correct, but scrollIntoView() had nothing visible to scroll to inside a zero-height container. Fixed with a new shared helper, _helpSetSectionVisibility(el, show), that sets all six properties identically everywhere a section's visibility is toggled — setHelpSection, _helpShowAllSections, and helpGoToResult now all call the same function.
    • FAQ & Getting Started — clarified that iOS reminder limitations are a platform restriction, not a CardiacLens bug. The existing "keep your screen on, Low Power Mode off" guidance explained what to do but not why, which read as CardiacLens simply not trying hard enough. Added an explicit comparison to Android: Chrome extends background service workers and Push API delivery to installed web apps even after the app is fully closed; iOS Safari suspends all background JavaScript for installed web apps the moment they leave the foreground, with no equivalent capability (iOS 16.4+ Web Push can deliver a notification but cannot run CardiacLens's reminder logic in the background). Matching Android's reliability on iOS would require a server and an account system, which would mean data leaving the device — CardiacLens prioritizes the serverless, account-free, on-device model instead. A companion note was added to Known Issues with the same explanation, framed as a permanent platform note rather than a fixed bug.
    🧪 New in v9.10.210 — June 12, 2026
    • Sentinel — 14-day sparkline and plain-language trend caption added to every stream card. Previously, each Sentinel stream (Pulse Pressure, HR Daily Spread, Symptom Frequency) showed only two numbers — "Your recent normal" vs. "Right now" — with a single status word (e.g. "STABLE"). This hid both volatility and direction: two numbers can look identical whether the underlying data is steady or swinging wildly day to day. Each card now includes a compact inline sparkline of the last 14 logged days with a dashed reference line at your recent normal, plus an auto-generated caption comparing the earlier vs. later half of that window against your personal day-to-day variability (e.g. "trending up... within ordinary noise" vs. "...worth keeping an eye on"). The caption is direction-aware per stream — for Pulse Pressure, widening is the favorable direction (CHF/diastolic dysfunction context); for HR Daily Spread and Symptom Frequency, narrowing/fewer is favorable. New helpers: _sentDailySeries(), _sentSparklineSVG(), _sentTrendCaption(), plus lookup tables SENTINEL_METRIC_FN and SENTINEL_GOOD_DIR. No new dependencies — the sparkline is hand-built inline SVG.
    🧪 New in v9.10.209 — June 12, 2026
    • Medication Intelligence Panel — search bar and floating back-to-top button. A sticky search field now sits at the top of the medicine list and filters cards live as you type, matching against medicine name or drug class (e.g. typing "beta" shows all Beta Blockers). Medicines are still grouped by drug class — matching groups stay together, and a "No medicines match your search" message appears if nothing matches. A small floating ⬆️ button appears in the bottom-right once you've scrolled down, and smoothly scrolls back to the top of the panel — useful on the longer medication lists. The search query is preserved across Approve/Update taps (which rebuild the panel) and clears when you close the panel. New helpers: _mipFilterMeds(), _mipSetupScroll(), _mipScrollTop().
    • Advanced Analytics — Doctor's Report no longer requires two taps to reach. Previously, generating a Doctor's Report meant tapping "Generate Analysis" first, then a "Generate Doctor's Report" button that only revealed a second section containing the real "Generate Doctor's Report" button — three taps total, even though report generation never actually depended on Generate Analysis having run. The two intermediate "reveal" buttons and the now-unused doctorReportSection panel have been removed. "🔍 Generate Analysis" and "🚚 Doctor's Report" now sit side by side under the date range selector — either can be tapped directly, in any order, and both use the same selected date range (or the last 30 days if none is selected).
    🧪 New in v9.10.208 — June 11, 2026
    • Medication Intelligence Panel — tapping Approve/Update no longer scrolls back to the top of the panel. Previously, every mipApprove() / mipApprovePP() tap rebuilt the entire panel and reset the scroll position to the very top (showModal() hardcodes scrollTop = 0), so reviewing several medicines in one sitting meant scrolling all the way back down after every single tap. Fixed with a new _mipPreserveScroll() helper: each medicine card and per-metric block now has a stable id; after the panel rebuilds, the same card/block is located again and the scroll position is restored relative to it — an exact element-anchored restore, not an estimate.
    • New: Weekly Threshold Review. Each approved threshold now records when it was last approved. When one or more medication thresholds (or the Pulse Pressure baseline) haven't been reviewed in 7+ days, a red action-item box appears on Today's Status — "💊 N medication threshold(s) due for weekly review." Tapping it opens a one-at-a-time review queue: for each due item you can use the data-driven suggestion, keep your current value, or edit manually, with the 7-day stats shown alongside both for context. A summary screen lists everything reviewed at the end. This replaces "scroll through everything and tap 20 buttons" with a short, linear checklist for the weekly review routine.
    • New: per-metric threshold history. Every approve/keep/edit action (from either the full panel or the new review queue) now appends a small breadcrumb — date, action taken, the suggestion at the time, and the before/after values — to a history array on that medicine/metric, capped at the last 12 entries. Nothing currently displays this yet; it's intended to feed Doctor's Report trend lines in a future version (e.g. "this threshold has held steady for 6 weeks" or "this threshold moved from X to Y over the past month").
    • Today's Status — new action-item box framework. The weekly review reminder is the first instance of a small reusable pattern: a stack of red/amber boxes above the zone summary for things that need attention, distinct from the existing status tiles. The stack only renders when there's something due, and disappears once cleared. Kept to this single use for now; other event types may route through it in a future version once this one has been used for a while.
    🧪 New in v9.10.207 — June 11, 2026
    • Medication Intelligence Panel — plain-language ranges added to Systolic, Diastolic, Heart Rate, and Pulse Pressure stat lines. Each "7-day: mean X, SD ±Y, n=Z" line now also shows "Averaging ~X, with most readings between (mean−1SD)–(mean+1SD)" in real units — the sentence you'd actually say to your cardiologist, translated from the underlying statistics. New _mipRangeText() helper; purely additive display, no changes to threshold calculations.
    • Caution acknowledgements (warn-tier "Log Anyway") are now recorded and correlated, like hard-block overrides already were. Previously, when a warn-level caution screen fired (rules warn, MIP warn, PP warn, HR warn) and the user tapped "Log Anyway — I Understand the Risk," nothing was recorded — the dose looked identical to a normal log entry, with no vitals-at-dose snapshot and no post-dose correlation. This meant the much more common "I see a warning but take it anyway" pattern produced zero feedback on whether proceeding helped or hurt. Fixed: _mipWarnProceed() now captures vitals-at-dose and a plain-language description of what triggered (new _warnDetailSummary()) into entry.warnProceed on the medLog entry — distinct from entry.override, since this is a caution acknowledgement, not a hard-block bypass. The existing 0–120 minute post-dose BP/HR correlation now runs on these too, surfaced in amber (vs. the override section's stronger styling) in three places: Medications log entries (today's log and history), Med Effectiveness ("Caution Acknowledgements" section per medicine), and Doctor's Report ("Caution Acknowledgement Log"). Ask context gains a parallel "MEDICATION CAUTION ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS TODAY" section so Ask can reason about these patterns too. This is a beta-only change — please log a few doses past warn screens and confirm the caution acknowledgement appears correctly with vitals and (after a follow-up BP reading) the post-dose correlation, before this goes to the live site.
    🧪 New in v9.10.206 — June 11, 2026
    • Log Blood Pressure — misaligned text cursor in input fields. On iOS, the blinking text cursor in the Systolic, Diastolic, and Heart Rate fields rendered taller than the entered text and extended below the field's border, looking visually broken even though the entered value was correct. Root cause: .modal-input had no explicit line-height, so the cursor used the browser's default line-height for the field's font size rather than one sized to match the text. Fixed by setting line-height:1.3 on .modal-input, which affects all modal text/number fields app-wide.
    • Log Weight — voice dictation of decimal weights (e.g. "180.2") only saved the whole-number part, and could leave a stray "e" in the field. Root cause: the weight field was type="number" with no sanitizer. iOS voice dictation writes its transcription directly into the field's value as it goes; for type="number" fields, the browser silently rejects (clears to empty, or truncates to the last valid number) any intermediate value that isn't a complete valid number — so a transcription that briefly passes through an invalid state (or includes a stray "e") can lose the decimal portion or leave "e" behind. Fixed: the Log Weight field (and the two weight-edit fields in Manage Weight History) are now type="text" inputmode="decimal", which still shows the numeric keypad but lets dictation write its full text without browser interference. A new _sanitizeDecimalInput() runs on onblur and strips any stray non-digit, non-decimal-point characters (such as "e") while preserving the decimal point and digits, so "180.2" is saved correctly.
    🧪 New in v9.10.205 — June 10, 2026
    • Automatic error logging (R19), and beta build banner. Two additions to the development process: (1) Any uncaught JavaScript error or unhandled promise rejection now sends a quiet GA4 event ("js_error" or "js_promise_rejection") with the error message, file, line number, and a short stack trace — up to 10 per session to avoid event storms. This means if something breaks for you (or any of CardiacLens's other users) on a real device, a record exists in the analytics dashboard before anyone has to notice, screenshot, or describe it. (2) The exact same code now also serves as a beta build: if CardiacLens is loaded from a URL containing "/beta", a purple banner reading "🧪 BETA BUILD" appears at the top of every screen, so it's always obvious which build is in use. Going forward, new versions will be deployed to the beta location first for real-world testing before being copied to the live site. Important: a beta deployment at cardiaclens.com/beta shares the same browser storage (your health data) as the main site, because they're the same website (just a different folder) — back up your data before testing a beta build, same as before any update.
    🧪 New in v9.10.204 — June 10, 2026
    • Real headless-browser execution testing added to the deploy process (R18), and a remaining gap it found was fixed. Every prior audit in this app's history checked the HTML/JS as text — syntax checks, string searches, version-string greps. None of that can detect what happens when the code actually runs. Starting with v9.10.204, every release is loaded in a real headless Chromium browser (Playwright) before being declared ready: the page is loaded with realistic data in localStorage, and the test confirms zero uncaught JavaScript errors. This immediately found a real remaining issue in the v9.10.203 chart-loading fix: if Chart.js fails to load entirely (not just a brief delay), the retry loop added in v9.10.203 (setTimeout(renderXChart, 100)) would retry forever with no fallback ever shown. Fixed: all 4 chart functions (BP, Fluid, HR, Anchor) now cap retries at 30 attempts (3 seconds); if Chart.js still hasn't loaded by then, BP Trends shows "Chart could not load. Pull down to refresh, or check your connection." instead of an indefinite silent retry, and the other 3 charts simply stay hidden without crashing. Headless Chromium does not perfectly replicate iOS Safari/WebKit — it cannot catch iOS-specific dictation or PWA-caching behavior — but it does catch the entire class of "uncaught error silently breaks app initialization" bugs, which is what caused the v9.10.198 and v9.10.201 incidents.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.203 — June 10, 2026
    • BP Trends, Fluid chart, HR chart, or Anchor Date charts can render blank. This is a pre-existing condition confirmed present (and code-identical) since at least v9.10.196 — it was not introduced by any change in v9.10.197–202. Likely root cause: CardiacLens loads Chart.js from a CDN using <script defer>, which by the HTML specification executes only after the entire document has finished parsing. The script that calls _startApp() (which in turn calls renderBPChart(), renderFluidChart(), createHRChart(), and buildAnchorCharts()) is a plain non-deferred <script> near the end of the document, which executes immediately as the parser reaches it — before the deferred Chart.js script has run. At that moment window.Chart is undefined, so new Chart(...) throws a ReferenceError. For BP Trends specifically, this happens after the "Need at least 2 readings" placeholder has already been hidden and the canvas already shown, so the result is a blank canvas with no message and no chart — matching what was reported. This explanation is the most likely mechanism based on the code structure, but has not been confirmed by reproducing the error in an actual browser console, since this environment cannot run the app live. Fixed defensively regardless: all 4 chart-creation functions (renderBPChart, renderFluidChart, createHRChart, buildAnchorCharts) now check typeof Chart==='undefined' at entry; if Chart.js hasn't executed yet, the function retries itself after 100ms instead of throwing. This is a no-op if Chart.js is already available, and self-heals the blank-chart condition if it isn't. If charts are still blank after this update, that would indicate a different cause and should be reported with the exact section affected.
    📋 Process improvement in v9.10.202 — June 10, 2026
    • New deploy rule R17 — Help documentation coverage sweep. Before any version is declared ready for GitHub upload, the keyword(s) describing that release's actual change are now grepped across all 19 Help tab sections (Welcome, Getting Started, How to Use, Med Intelligence, Deep Dive, Pinned Events, Sentinel, Ask, Morning Check-In, Breathing Logger, Heat Advisory, Post-Meal BP, FAQ, Privacy, Data Mgmt, Known Issues, Planned Features, Support, Glossary). For v9.10.201 (the voice/dictation fix), the keywords "microphone", "dictat", "voice", and "speech" were swept across all 19 tabs: only Known Issues contained relevant content (the v9.10.201 entry itself), and the FAQ's "Can I use voice dictation?" entry — which is generated separately by renderFAQ() and isn't part of the static section sweep — was independently confirmed updated. No other tab required changes. This sweep, including which tabs matched and which didn't, is now a required part of the R14 pre-zip audit output for every future release.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.201 — June 10, 2026
    • Voice dictation broken on numeric fields, and "field is empty" prompts repeat after dictating. The v9.10.197/198 fix for the stray-"e" bug used oninput="_sanitizeNumericInput(this)", which fires on every keystroke and on every partial update during active iOS voice dictation. Synchronously rewriting an input's .value while iOS dictation is mid-session desyncs the field from the dictation engine, which can stop dictation from committing any text at all. When that happens the field is left empty, so any "Please enter a value" validation correctly (but frustratingly) keeps re-firing — the field really is empty, but only because dictation never landed. This affected all 72 numeric fields that received the v9.10.197/198 fix, including BP, HR, breathing logger, fluid amounts, durations, thresholds, and history filter ranges. Fixed: the sanitizer now runs on onblur (when you tap away from the field) instead of oninput. This lets dictation complete normally and commit its full text, then cleans up any stray non-digit characters once you're done with the field — preserving the original fix's goal (no stray "e" in saved BP/HR values) without interfering with the dictation session itself. The 12 history filter range fields (which also call a live filter-update function on every keystroke) keep that live oninput behavior unchanged and gained a separate onblur sanitizer.
    ✨ Improved in v9.10.200 — June 10, 2026
    • PWA update instructions rewritten — try close-and-reopen first. Previously, the "See What's New" modal and the Settings → App Version & Updates panel told Home Screen icon users that updating always required deleting and re-adding the icon (with a mandatory backup gate first). This was accurate guidance for the older cache-first service worker (used through v9.10.150 and earlier), where the cached app shell would never refresh on its own. The current service worker (since the v9.10.196 rebuild) uses a network-first fetch strategy — every request tries the live server first and only falls back to the cache when offline. Under this strategy, simply force-closing CardiacLens (swipe it away in the app switcher) and reopening it from the Home Screen icon often picks up the new version directly, with zero risk to local data, since localStorage is untouched either way. The update modal and Settings panel now present this as the first thing to try, with the backup-and-reinstall flow retained as a clearly-labeled fallback if the version number doesn't change after two reopen attempts. This has not yet been confirmed across all iOS versions — if close-and-reopen does not update the version after two tries, the original backup/reinstall method remains fully available and unchanged.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.199 — June 10, 2026
    • App frozen on splash screen after install (JavaScript syntax error). v9.10.198 was uploaded to GitHub but would not load on mobile or Chromebook — the splash screen progress bar reached ~85% and froze permanently. Root cause: the automated regex that injected oninput="_sanitizeNumericInput(this)" onto numeric fields in v9.10.197/198 had a flaw when the target field was built across a JS string concatenation containing a ternary expression. In those cases (bpFluid, fluidAmount, mealFluid, flwMinA), the regex injected the attribute outside the closing quote of the string literal and inside the ternary — producing broken JS like (presetFluid oninput="...">0?... instead of valid HTML. This is a silent failure: the HTML renders visually correct but the JS syntax error prevents the entire script block from executing, so the app init function never fires. Fixed: all 4 broken injections corrected manually; JS syntax verified clean with node --check. New rules R7/R7a added to the deploy checklist requiring a mandatory JS syntax check after any automated HTML patching, and R14 updated to include this check in the pre-zip audit.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.197/198 — June 10, 2026
    • Missed-Event Catch-Up: not appearing on app reopen. When an event time passed while the screen was off, reopening the app showed the normal dashboard with the event marked overdue, but the new "Missed While Screen Was Off" recovery screen never appeared. Root cause: on iOS, reopening a suspended (not fully terminated) PWA from the home screen restores the page via the pageshow BFCache event, not a fresh page load. The catch-up scan was only wired into cold-start initialization and the visibilitychange handler — not the pageshow restore path, which is the most common way the app is reopened on iPhone. Fixed: catch-up scan now also runs 800ms after a BFCache pageshow restore.
    • Voice dictation inserting "e" into numeric fields (BP, HR, and all integer number fields). When using iOS speech-to-text to dictate a number into any numeric field (systolic, diastolic, heart rate, breathing logger HR, history filter ranges, medication thresholds, and all other integer inputs), the field sometimes received a lowercase "e" instead of or alongside the spoken number. Root cause: the existing keydown blocker correctly filtered keyboard entry of e/E on type="number" fields, but iOS voice dictation bypasses the keyboard entirely — it writes the transcribed text directly into the field value, never firing a keydown event. Fixed: a new _sanitizeNumericInput() function strips any non-digit character on every oninput event, covering voice, paste, autofill, and all non-keyboard input paths. Applied to all 59 integer numeric fields across the app. The keydown blocker was also extended to cover inputmode="decimal" fields (fluid and weight filter ranges, med fluid entry) which block e/E/+/- via keyboard while still allowing the decimal point. Free-text fields (meal descriptions, notes, portions, serving sizes) are unaffected and continue to accept full voice dictation including phrases like "1/4 cup" or "8 oz".
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.190 — June 8, 2026
    • Notifications — scheduled event reminders not firing. The checkEventReminders function filtered out any settings.dailyEvents entry whose reminderEnabled property was undefined or absent (falsy check), rather than only skipping entries explicitly set to false. Events created via certain code paths did not persist a reminderEnabled:true field, causing them to be silently skipped on every poll. Fixed: guard changed from !evt.reminderEnabled to evt.reminderEnabled===false so only deliberately disabled events are excluded. Additionally wrapped the entire function in try/catch so a runtime error cannot silently stop future ticks.
    • Ask — Nutritional Summary heading displayed raw unicode escape. The Today’s Nutritional Summary heading in the Ask context nutritional panel was rendered as the literal text \U0001f4ca instead of the 📊 emoji. Fixed: replaced the malformed escape with the emoji character directly.
    • Ask — Food Check label fields overflow on mobile. The Serving / Sodium / Potassium / Phosphorus label inputs in Food Check were laid out in a single 4-column row (grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr), causing the columns to be too narrow on phone screens and overflowing the panel width. Changed to a 2×2 grid (grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr) matching the fix already applied to Meal Builder in v9.10.160.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.189 — June 6, 2026
    • Medication safety — HR warn firing on single spike reading (Step 9). The HR warn in Step 9 of the safety gate used the average of the last 3 HR readings and fired if that average exceeded the threshold. A single elevated reading could pull the average over the threshold even when the other two readings were well within range — producing a warn that did not reflect a sustained pattern. Changed to a majority rule: the warn now requires 2 of 3 recent HR readings to individually exceed the threshold. The warn screen text is updated to reflect this: it now shows how many of the 3 readings are above the ceiling rather than reporting the average. The aboveCount field is included in the return object for display and future logging use.
    • Medication safety — MIP thresholds not synced into condition rules for medicines approved before v9.10.187. The v9.10.187 sync code only ran going forward when a threshold was approved in the MIP panel. Any medicine whose thresholds were approved before that code existed retained an empty conditionRules array, meaning the rule engine evaluated those medicines as having no conditions — only the separate MIP evaluation path applied. On a reading where the rule engine passes but MIP fires, the medicine received a warn from the MIP path while other medicines with synced rules showed nothing — producing inconsistent and confusing warn behavior. Fixed with a one-time migration at app load (guarded by cardiaclens_mip_sync_v189_done flag): for every active medicine, any MIP metric (systolic, diastolic, HR, PP) that has an approved threshold but no corresponding conditionRule is upserted as a block rule and warn rule into conditionRules, matching the behavior of a freshly approved threshold. The migration only adds rules — it never modifies or removes rules the user set manually. conditionNote is regenerated after sync. Affected medicines in this backup: Eliquis 5mg (all 4 metrics missing), Farxiga 10mg (HR, PP missing), Mounjaro 2.5ml (HR, PP missing), Trazodone 100mg (HR, PP missing).
    ✨ New in v9.10.188 — June 6, 2026
    • Post-override BP/HR correlation. When a medication dose is logged via override (i.e. the safety gate was bypassed with a documented reason), CardiacLens now automatically correlates BP and HR readings logged within 0–120 minutes after that dose. Three locations surface this data: (1) Doctor's Report — the Medication Override Log now shows a green post-override window card per entry, displaying average post-dose BP/HR and the delta from vitals-at-dose (green = favorable direction, red = adverse); if no post-dose readings exist a note says so. (2) Med Effectiveness — each medication card now includes an amber override-dose section listing every override in the analysis period with dose-time vitals and post-dose window averages where available. (3) Ask context — the MEDICATION OVERRIDES TODAY section now includes post-override BP/HR averages and deltas so Ask can reason about whether an override dose produced a favorable or adverse hemodynamic response.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.187 — June 3, 2026
    • Medication Intelligence Panel — MIP auto-sync with condition rules. When a threshold is approved in the Medication Intelligence Panel, CardiacLens now automatically creates or updates the corresponding condition rules on the medicine record. Previously, MIP-approved thresholds were stored in a separate data store and evaluated independently of condition rules — meaning a medicine could appear to have "no condition rules" in Manage Medicines even when active MIP thresholds were protecting it, and threshold conflicts between the two systems could produce inconsistent block/warn behavior. The fix: mipApprove() now upserts a block rule at the block threshold and a warn rule at the warn threshold directly into the medicine's conditionRules array. If a rule for that metric already exists, its threshold is updated in-place while preserving the user-set outcome (block or warn). All MIP-synced rules are labelled MIP-approved (date) so they are distinguishable from manually entered rules. MIP-approved PP thresholds now also sync a PP warn rule across all active medicines that do not already have a user-defined PP rule. The conditionNote on each medicine is regenerated after sync so the MIP panel, Manage Medicines, and the log-time safety check all reflect the same state. Help & About updated: the "You Approve Everything" section and the Condition Rules section now accurately describe the auto-sync behavior.
    • Medication Safety — Multi-medicine warn acknowledgement broken (infinite loop). When logging multiple medicines together, any warn screen (MIP warn, PP warn, HR warn, rules warn) was trapped in an infinite loop: _mipWarnProceed() set the proceed-flag to true then called saveMedicine(), but saveMedicine() immediately reset the flag to false because the sentinel argument 'warned' was never actually passed. Result: the same warn screen appeared again and again — logging could never complete after any warn acknowledgement. Fixed: _mipWarnProceed() now correctly passes 'warned' to saveMedicine(). Additionally, warn acknowledgement is now tracked per-medicine per save pass using _mipWarnAckedMeds — each medicine in a multi-selection gets its own warn screen shown exactly once, and no warn is silently bypassed when multiple medicines are logged together. Affects all users logging multiple medicines simultaneously with any warn condition active.
    ✨ New in v9.10.175 — June 3, 2026
    • Ask CardiacLens — Document Attachment. A 📎 attach button now appears in the Ask input bar. Tap it to attach up to 2 files (PDF, JPG, or PNG — up to 5 MB each) before sending a question. Attached files are sent to Claude alongside your CardiacLens health data so you can ask questions directly referencing a hospital discharge summary, lab report, or specialist letter. A preview strip above the input shows attached filenames with a remove option. The blue dot on the button confirms a file is loaded. Claude focuses only on the parts of the document relevant to your specific question — it does not reproduce the full document. Attachments are session-only and never stored anywhere. Timeout extended to 35 seconds when documents are attached. Help & About Ask tab updated with full attachment documentation.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.175 — June 3, 2026
    • Help & About — MIP drug class header styling and GLP-1 lookup from v9.10.173 carried forward. Version increment only — no new changes.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.175 — June 3, 2026
    • Help & About — tabs not switching (pre-existing corruption fixed). The Planned Features section had a missing opening <div> on one entry, leaving a stale </div> that closed a parent container early. This broke the DOM structure of every section after it — JavaScript’s setHelpSection hide/show calls could not reach the displaced content. Present since v9.10.32. Fixed by inserting the missing wrapper div. All 18 help sections verified balanced by automated div audit before release.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.175 — June 3, 2026
    • Medication Intelligence Panel — drug class headers now clearly visible. Class group headers were styled in a subtle grey that was easy to miss. Updated to bold dark blue text on a blue-tinted background with a left accent bar, matching the visual weight of the section they introduce. All class groups now have a header — medicines with no class assigned previously rendered with no group label. They now appear under an “Other / Unclassified” header.
    • Drug class lookup — GLP-1 Agonists added. Mounjaro (tirzepatide), Ozempic, Wegovy, Victoza, Trulicity, and all common GLP-1 agonists added to the auto-detection table. Any medicine in this class will now have its drug class auto-suggested correctly on load and on new entry.
    ✨ New in v9.10.175 — June 3, 2026
    • Medication Safety — Multi-Condition Rule Engine. Each medicine now supports up to 4 condition rules replacing the previous single-metric check. Each rule specifies a metric (Systolic, Diastolic, Heart Rate, or Pulse Pressure), a direction (above/below), a threshold value, an outcome (Block or Warn), and an optional reason note. Multiple rules can be evaluated with OR logic (any rule triggers) or AND logic (all must be true simultaneously). A preview line shows the full rule set in plain English before saving. Legacy single-condition medicines are automatically migrated to the new format on first load — no data is lost.
    • Medication Safety — Rule Builder UI. The Manage Medicines Add/Edit screen now shows a dynamic rule card builder replacing the old single-metric dropdowns. Each rule is a self-contained card with metric, direction, value, and outcome selectors. Rules can be added (up to 4) and removed individually. The logic selector (OR/AND) appears automatically when 2 or more rules exist. A live preview updates as rules are changed.
    • Medication Safety — Block and Warn screens updated. When a block or warn triggers, the screen now shows exactly which rule(s) fired, the actual measured value versus the threshold, and the reason note for each triggered rule. MIP-sourced blocks are now labelled clearly. A new Rules Warn screen handles multi-rule warn outcomes.
    • Drug Class field added to medicines. Each medicine can now be tagged with a drug class (Beta Blocker, ARB, ACE Inhibitor, etc.). The class is auto-suggested from the medicine name using a built-in lookup table — the user confirms or changes it. Drug class is used in the Medication Intelligence Panel for grouped display and to offer a copy-rules shortcut when two medicines share the same class.
    • Medication Intelligence Panel — expanded scope. The MIP now shows all active medicines, not only those with condition rules. Medicines are grouped by drug class. Each medicine card shows its active condition rules and offers threshold suggestions for all four metrics (Systolic, Diastolic, HR, Pulse Pressure) where data exists. A copy-rules button appears when another medicine of the same class already has rules configured.
    • Ask CardiacLens — medication rules added to context. A new MEDICATION SAFETY RULES section is included in every Ask context, showing the active condition rules and any MIP-approved thresholds for each medicine. Ask can now answer questions about what rules are set and why a dose may have been blocked or warned.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.175 — June 3, 2026
    • Help & About — tab switching broken on iPhone (root cause found). The right scroll chevron overlay div (tabScrollRight) was position:absolute, z-index:10, spanning full height of the tab bar, always visible (display:flex). On iPhone it permanently covered the first 52px from the right edge of the tab bar, intercepting all touch events on Getting Started, How to Use, and other nearby tabs. Fixed: overlay div set to pointer-events:none so touch passes through to tabs beneath; the arrow span inside gets pointer-events:auto so scrolling still works when the arrow itself is tapped.
    • Help & About — section content bleeding on iOS. On iOS Safari with -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch, display:none alone does not fully prevent hidden sections from painting. Fixed: visibility:hidden, height:0, overflow:hidden, and zeroed padding/margin added alongside display:none in setHelpSection for non-matching sections.
    • Help & About — planned-features section incorrectly nested inside known-issues. An extra nesting level caused planned-features to render inside known-issues rather than as a sibling section. Fixed: section now correctly placed as a sibling.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.170 — June 3, 2026
    • Help & About — scroll and tab switching on iPhone (second pass). v9.10.169 fix incorrectly set overflow:hidden on .help-modal-content inside the #aboutModal override — reintroducing the same iOS touch-blocking condition it was trying to fix. Changed to overflow:visible. Also added touch-action:manipulation to both scroll chevron overlay divs which were positioned absolutely above the tab bar and intercepting tap events on iOS even when hidden.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.169 — June 3, 2026
    • Help & About — scroll and tab switching broken on iPhone (root cause). The .modal CSS class sets overflow:hidden on the backdrop element. On iOS Safari, overflow:hidden on a parent intercepts all touch events, preventing child elements from receiving scroll or tap gestures regardless of their own overflow settings. Fixed with a dedicated #aboutModal CSS rule overriding to overflow-y:auto with -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch. Same property added to .modal-body and .hr-tabs via the #aboutModal rule block. Inline styles on both elements also updated to be consistent.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.168 — June 3, 2026
    • Help & About — tab scroll and content scroll broken on iPhone. openHelpModal rewrites the tab bar style.cssText via a setTimeout, which wiped out the CSS-applied -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch rule. Without this property on iOS, touch momentum scrolling was broken — tabs could not be swiped and content could not scroll. Fixed: -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch!important added to the inline cssText override. Also corrected padding-right to 44px so tabs can scroll past the right chevron overlay.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.167 — June 3, 2026
    • Medication Intelligence — approve button messaging after approval. Once thresholds were approved, the green ✅ Approve & Activate button remained visible below the Currently Approved badge, creating a contradictory message. Fixed: when approved thresholds exist, the button changes to a grey outlined ✏️ Update Thresholds style — clearly secondary, reads as "modify if needed" rather than "action required." Applies to both medicine thresholds and PP thresholds.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.166 — June 3, 2026
    • Help & About — Med Intelligence tab button missing from tab bar. The htab-med-intel button was added to the JavaScript tabKeys arrays and the section HTML was created, but the actual <button> element was never inserted into the HTML tab bar. The tab was unreachable by tapping. Fixed: button inserted before the Deep Dive tab.
    • Help & About — modal opening to previously-visited tab instead of Welcome. When opened from the main page Help & About button, the tab bar scrolled to the previously-active tab rather than resetting to the start. Fixed: when opening to welcome, tab bar scrollLeft is explicitly reset to 0 so the first tabs are always visible on open.
    ✨ New in v9.10.165 — June 3, 2026
    • Medication Intelligence Panel. New panel accessible from the main page (🧠 Medication Intelligence button) that computes personalized block and warn thresholds for each active medication using 7, 30, and 90-day windows of your own BP, HR, and pulse pressure data. Thresholds are derived using mean − 1 SD (block) and mean − 0.5 SD (warn) — a method validated in peer-reviewed hypertension literature. Nothing activates until you review and approve. Storage key: CARDIACLENS_MED_INTEL. Approved thresholds survive data imports and are reloaded automatically.
    • Dual-condition medication safety — warn states. The condition check system now supports three new return types alongside the existing hard block: mip_warn (metric in caution zone per MIP thresholds), pp_warn (current pulse pressure below approved PP warn threshold), and hr_warn (average of last 3 HR readings above target ceiling). Each surfaces a distinct amber caution screen — the user must acknowledge before proceeding, but is not hard-blocked. The MIP warn proceed flag resets at the start of each new save attempt.
    • Help & About — Medication Intelligence tab added. New help section covering the three-window data display, the mean−SD threshold calculation methodology, the approve-before-activate workflow, PP tracking, and the clinical research basis for the approach.
    • Pinned Events help — manual pin documented. Help section updated to cover the new ✏️ Add button, form fields, and use cases for manual pins.
    • Medication Safety System help — warn states documented. Condition Warning, Pulse Pressure Warning, and Elevated Heart Rate Warning sections added to the safety system help.
    ✨ New in v9.10.164 — June 3, 2026
    • Pinned Events — Manual Pin creation. Users can now create pins directly from the Pinned Events modal without needing a detector finding. Tap the new ✏️ Add button in the modal header to open an inline form with fields for Title (required), Date (required), Summary/Notes, and Doctor. Saved pins appear as grey cards with a ✏️ Manual Entry label and support all existing pin features — communication log, question list, status tracking, and Doctor's Report inclusion. Previously, pins could only be created by automated detectors (Sentinel, HR, PPH, Ask findings).
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.163 — June 3, 2026
    • Saved Meals — meals lost from library after data import. After importing a backup, _confirmImport() reloaded all other data but never reloaded the savedMeals in-memory array from localStorage. The stale pre-import array remained active, and any subsequent meal save would overwrite localStorage with that stale array, silently erasing meals that were in the backup. Fixed: savedMeals is now explicitly reloaded from localStorage immediately after all other post-import reloads.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.162 — June 1, 2026
    • Meal Builder — meal name and type persisting across sessions. When opening Meal Builder fresh (no pending meal), mbItems was cleared but the mbMealName and mbMealType DOM fields were never reset. Previous session's meal name and type remained visible. Fixed: both fields are now explicitly cleared in the fresh-start branch of openMealBuilder.
    🐛 Fixed in v9.10.162 — June 1, 2026
    • Ask CardiacLens — blank/non-responsive on reopen after using Meal Builder. When Meal Builder or Food Check was open, askThread was set to display:none. The v9.10.160 fix hid the tool panels on reopen but did not restore askThread to visible — leaving Ask blank with no input response. Fixed: openAskPanel now explicitly restores askThread to display:flex alongside resetting the tool panels.
    • Ask CardiacLens — Meal Builder label fields overflowing on mobile. The Serving / Sodium / Potassium / Phosphorus label value inputs were laid out in a single 4-column row (grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr), causing the fields to be too narrow to use on a phone screen. Changed to a 2×2 grid (grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr) so all four fields are legible and tappable.
    • Medicine History filter dropdown — source corrected. The Medicine filter in Medicines History (90 Days) was built from logged history entries instead of the medicine list. This caused two problems: discontinued medicines appeared as filter options (they have historical log entries), and newly added medicines were missing (they had no entries yet). Fixed: the dropdown now sources from settings.medicineList (active medicines only), then appends any historical medicine names from log entries not already in the list. Discontinued medicines are excluded from the dropdown.
    🔧 New in v9.10.162 — May 31, 2026
    • Ask CardiacLens — Food Check. A structured food evaluation form added to the Ask welcome screen. Tap “🥦 Check a food or product label” to open a form with fields for product name, serving size, sodium, potassium, and phosphorus (optional — Ask uses its own knowledge when not on the label). On submit, Ask evaluates the food against the patient’s logged conditions and current medication list and returns a one-line verdict (✅ Safe, ⚠️ Caution, or 🚫 Avoid) with a brief explanation specific to the patient’s profile. No new UI section, no new data storage — fully integrated into the existing Ask infrastructure.
    • Medication Safety System — Steps 1 & 2: Data model + Condition Check UI. Three new optional fields added to the medicine object: conditionMetric (systolic / diastolic / hr / null), conditionThreshold (number or null), conditionDirection (above / below / null). A new “Condition Check” section now appears at the bottom of the Add/Edit medicine screen — select Diastolic, Systolic, or Heart Rate, enter a threshold, and choose whether to block when the reading is below or above that threshold. Selecting None clears all condition fields. Existing medicines without these fields are unaffected.
    • Time selection — over-selection bug fixed. Selecting more checkboxes than the allowed number of daily doses (e.g. checking 2 boxes for a once-daily medicine) now automatically unchecks the earliest selection, keeping the count at the correct limit. Previously the UI allowed over-selection and only caught it on Save.
    🔧 Fixed in v9.10.162 — May 28, 2026
    • Log Medicine — conditional note now visible at the moment of logging. Medicines with a conditionNote (e.g. “Only when diastolic >70” or “Only when systolic >100”) now display an amber warning banner directly under the schedule note in the Log Medicine modal. Previously the condition note only appeared in Manage Medicines, meaning it was invisible at the critical moment of deciding whether to take the medication. Affects Metoprolol, Losartan, and any medicine with a clinical condition attached. Medicines whose conditionNote is simply “As needed” are unaffected since the schedule line already conveys this.
    🔧 Updated in v9.10.126 — May 14, 2026
    • × Close FAB — now only appears on #modal, not on dedicated modals. The FAB was appearing on Advanced Analytics, Medication Effectiveness, and other dedicated modals that have their own close buttons. Since hideModal() only closes #modal, tapping the FAB on those screens did nothing while covering their real close button. Fix: _syncFab() now checks whether #modal specifically is open rather than any element with class modal. The FAB now only appears on logging forms and other content rendered inside #modal.
    • × Close FAB — repositioned to top:24px right:18px. Previously at bottom left, the FAB overlapped the Cancel and Save buttons on mobile. Moved to top right corner where it is out of the way of all form content and clearly distinct from bottom action buttons.
    • Wrapping buttons made full width. Six buttons in the Analytics and Planning sections had labels too long for a two-column grid at 17px on mobile screens: HR Rate Detector, Advanced Analytics, Med Effectiveness, Procedure Impact, Plan My Day, and Log Appointment. Each now uses grid-column:1/-1 to span the full container width. The pattern already existed on Decompensation Warning, Pulse Pressure Detector, Symptom Convergence, Sentinel, and Pinned Events.
    • HR Stat Card text alignment fixed. The .hr-stat-card CSS rule contained text-center; — a Tailwind class name accidentally written as a CSS property. It is not valid CSS and was silently ignored, leaving stat card text left-aligned. Fixed to text-align:center;.
    • Current Status card labels shortened. “Today’s Avg” and “Today’s Range” labels were wider than the 3-column card grid allowed on narrow screens, causing “65-82” and “6 readings” to appear on adjacent visual lines. Labels shortened to “Avg HR” and “Range”.
    • Wizard checklist strikethrough removed. Completed checklist items were wrapped in <s> tags, displaying as struck-through text. On a medical checklist this reads as deleted rather than accomplished. Fix: completed items now display in bold with a ✅ checkmark and green background only — no strikethrough.
    • Fluid card “text us” removed. The fluid entries card contained the phrase “Ask your doctor if you would benefit from a fluid chart view and text us.” The phrase “and text us” was leftover development text with no contact information. Removed.
    • Scroll event listener — passive flag added. The tab chevron scroll listener on #helpTabBar was registered without {passive:true}. Non-passive scroll listeners block the browser’s scroll thread and are flagged by Lighthouse. Fix: listener now registered with {passive:true}. The touchstart listener was already correctly marked passive.
    • Guided Setup Wizard — “Save My Checklist” button fixed for Path A. Path A’s final screen called _wzSaveChecklist() which was never defined. Paths B, C, and D correctly called _wzSaveChecklistPath(pathId). Fix: Path A now calls _wzSaveChecklistPath(‘A’) matching the pattern used by all other paths. The confirmation alert (“✓ Checklist saved!”) now appears correctly after tapping Save My Checklist on Path A.
    • Audio alert — pulse animation fixed. The .audio-alert CSS rule was missing its closing } before @keyframes pulse. The keyframe block was nested inside the unclosed rule, which is invalid CSS. Browsers silently discarded the animation declaration, leaving the alert box static when it should pulse.
